title: Combustion grate for an incinerator of a waste incineration plant
abstract: A combustion grate comprising a tensioning device (30) for a row (16) of grate blocks (18) arranged in the combustion grate (10), the tensioning device (30) being designed to maintain the alignment of the row of grate blocks. The tensioning device comprises a cable (70) having a cable first end region (70a) and a cable second end region (70b) opposed to the cable first end region (70a), a first connecting means (40) for connecting the cable first end region (70a) to a side wall (14a) of a first border region of the combustion grate bordering laterally the combustion grate, and a second connecting means (50) for connecting the cable second end region (70b) to a side wall (14b) of a second border region opposed to the first border region and bordering laterally the combustion grate.
claims:
Claims Claims 1. Combustion grate (10) for an incinerator of a waste incineration plant, the combustion grate (10) having a first border region and a second border region opposed to the first border region, said first border region and second border region bordering laterally the combustion grate (10) and extending in a direction parallel to a direction of conveyance (12) of the combustion grate, a row of grate blocks extending between the first border region and the second border region, the combustion grate comprising a tensioning device (30) for the row (16) of grate blocks (18) arranged in the combustion grate (10) , the tensioning device (30) extending in a longitudinal direction and being designed to maintain the alignment of the row of grate blocks, characterized in that the tensioning device comprises a cable (70) having a cable first end region (70a) and a cable second end region (70b) opposed to the cable first end region (70a) , a first connecting means (40) for connecting the cable first end region (70a) to a side wall (14a) of the first border region, and a second connecting means (50) for connecting the cable second end region (70b) to a side wall (14b) of the second border region. 2. Combustion grate (10) according to claim 1, characterized in that the first border region and the second border region are formed by press plates (15a, 15b) of the 

 combustion grate that extend parallel to the direction of conveyance (12) , or in that the first border region and the second border region are formed by a first side grate block (20a) and a second side grate block (20b) , respectively, the first side grate block (20a) forming a first end of the row and the second side grate block (20b) forming a second end of the row opposed to the first end of the row. 3. Combustion grate (10) according to claim 1 or 2, characterized in that an end of the first connecting means (40) that faces away from the cable and/or an end of the second connecting means (50) that faces away from the cable is formed as a foot plate, the foot plate being configured to be inserted in a similar shaped hole in the side wall of the first border region and/or the side wall of the second border region, respectively, and to be subsequently rotated by an angle, preferably of 90Â°, so as to rest on the surface of the side wall facing away from the cable (70) and to be retained in a mounted state of the row (16) of grate blocks (18) . 4. Combustion grate (10) according to any one of claims 1 or 2, characterized in that the first connecting means (40) or the second connecting means (50) is welded to the side wall of the first border region or the side wall of the second border region, respectively. 5. Combustion grate (10) according to any one of claims 1 to 4, characterized in that the first connecting means (40) and/or the second connecting means (50) comprise a cable fitting (82, 86) configured to be connected in the mounted 

 state with the cable first end region (70a) and/or the cable second end region (70b) , respectively. 6. Combustion grate (10) according to claim 5, characterized in that the cable fitting (82, 86) comprises a threaded section formed at the end of the cable fitting opposed to the cable, and in that the first connecting means (40) and/or the second connection (50) means comprises a receiving threaded section corresponding to the threaded section to attach the cable fitting (82, 86) , the receiving threaded section being preferably in the form of a rod having a threaded longitudinal hole. 7. Combustion grate (10) according to any one of claims 5 to 6, characterized in that the cable fitting (82, 86) has two parallel surfaces (90) arranged diametrically opposed with respect to a longitudinal axis of the tensioning device for torque transmission. 8. Combustion grate (10) according to any one of claims 1 to 7, characterized by a tensioning means to adjust the tension of the cable (70) in the mounted state of the row of grate blocks (18) . 9. Combustion grate (10) according to claim 8, characterized in that the tensioning means is a turnbuckle (60) . 10. Combustion grate (10) according to any one of claims 5 to 7 and any one of claims 8 to 9, characterized in that the first connecting means or the second connecting means comprises a main body distinct from the cable fitting and connected in the mounted state to the side wall (14a) of the first border region or to the side wall (14b) of the 

 second border region, respectively, and the tensioning means is arranged between the cable fitting and the main body . 11. Combustion grate (10) according to claim 8, characterized in that the tensioning means is formed as a threaded portion formed in an end region of the first connecting means (40) that faces away from the cable (70) or in an end region of the second connecting means (50) that faces away from the cable (70) , wherein the threaded portion extends in the mounted state of the row of grate blocks (18) through an opening in the side wall (14a) of the first border region or an opening in the side wall (14b) of the second border region, respectively, and the threaded portion is retained by at least one nut on each side of the side wall. 12. Combustion grate (10) according to any one of claims 1 to 11, characterized in that the cable (70) has a diameter of at least 6.0 mm, more preferably between 8.0 mm and 25.0 mm, most preferably between 10.0 mm and 15.0 mm. 13. Combustion grate (10) according to any one of claims 1 to 12, characterized in that the cable (70) is made of stainless steel. 14. Combustion grate (10) according to any one of claims 1 to 13, characterized in that the cable (70) has at least three strands. 15. Combustion grate (10) according to any one of claims 1 to 14, characterized in that the cable (70) extends 

continuously from the cable first end region (70a) to the cable second end region (70b) .
